grid.label: 37 OKAFOR WALK
grid.player_desc: Arthur Okafor, 72, has walked this route every morning for forty-one years. He got lost here. His dog sat down and would not move. First data point CAMPBELL flagged.
grid.keeper_note: Arthur's starting point. The distortion hits here first each morning. His dog is fine — animals are immune to the effect. Arthur will help if found.

grid.label: 14 ALDERMOOR CRESCENT
grid.player_desc: Residence of Margaret Holt, 74. CAMPBELL flagged this address as the topographic distortion epicentre. Uncontactable for 9 days at time of briefing.
grid.keeper_note: The Cartographer is centred here. The excavation breach is directly below the garden. Within 20 feet the cognitive map fails completely. Margaret is inside — disoriented but alive.

grid.label: SITE OFFICE
grid.player_desc: A temporary structure at the edge of the sealed excavation site. String lines extend from it in four directions. Someone has been using it as a base.
grid.keeper_note: Diane Marsh's shelter. She's been here 9 days — systematic, calm, and she has a notebook. She knows the entity isn't hostile. She will help if approached correctly.

grid.label: ALDERMOOR PARK
grid.player_desc: A small park on the west edge of the district. Arthur's morning route passes along its back fence. Animals move through it without hesitation. The distortion feels weakest here.
grid.keeper_note: Clearest area in the district mid-morning. Cognitive map stays intact here. Useful staging point — the effect weakens between 10am and noon.

grid.label: FORMER RESERVOIR
grid.player_desc: A former water reservoir, partially excavated and then abruptly sealed. Orange fencing. Construction equipment abandoned mid-work. Something about the ground here feels wrong.
grid.keeper_note: The entity is distributed through the substrate here. The excavation breach is what woke it. Diane's threshold sketch is visible at the base of the dig. This is where contact is possible.

grid.label: THE POSTBOX CORNER
grid.player_desc: The corner where Arthur's morning route turns right. The postbox was gone — then back. Chalk arrows left by residents point in multiple contradictory directions.
grid.keeper_note: Distortion peaks here. Cognitive map snaps when you try to hold the junction in mind. At least two residents tried to mark safe routes. The arrows don't agree.

grid.label: ALDERMOOR PROPERTY SERVICES
grid.player_desc: Closed and locked. CAMPBELL has flagged 47 property acquisitions across the district in 18 months — three shell companies, all registered in Jersey. Someone still has a key.
grid.keeper_note: Whiteboard inside has the acquisition log. Front door locked. Side window: faulty latch. Recent muddy boot print — Rook has been here. Helix Boundary Ltd connection.

nodes.label: ST. BARNABAS HOSPITAL
nodes.player_desc: Two hospitalised understudies. Ward C. Visiting hours flexible if you seem official enough.
nodes.keeper_note: Route A. Edmund (in Daniel) is here — cooperative, warm, emotional centrepiece. Clara's notebook location: Edmund-as-Daniel knows. Third hospitalised understudy is in a different hospital — treat as extension of this node.

nodes.label: MERIDIAN THEATRE
nodes.player_desc: Regional repertory company. Stage door at the back. Petra Novak (stage manager) is the gatekeeper — cooperative if you seem professional.
nodes.keeper_note: Hub node. Entry point to all interior locations. Petra is here. The fey watcher (Alan's seed) is visible from the street near the stage door during Scene 1.

nodes.label: THEATRE FOYER
nodes.player_desc: Front-of-house. Anniversary season display. A 19th-century prompt book in a glass case.
nodes.keeper_note: Prompt book here — unlocked, unalarmed. Rex's scanner: significantly elevated BIM — highest fixed-object reading in building. Grant acquisition appendix lists it. The mechanism's physical source.

nodes.label: BACKSTAGE
nodes.player_desc: Her name is still on the door. The room has been used for storage but her mirror and makeup table are still there.
nodes.keeper_note: Highest BIM reading in the building. Sven alone: Clara makes contact briefly. Alan alone or with Sven: fey visitor's trace is legible. Key for both Sven arc seed and Alan's fey seed. Clara knows MESA is here — she's been watching.

nodes.label: GREEN ROOM
nodes.player_desc: Between rehearsal sections. Four understudies still performing. Sophie Marsh is the most forthcoming.
nodes.keeper_note: Route B. Understudies are fully themselves here but they've noticed things. Sophie Marsh: frightened, articulate, already changing (Rex's scanner: partial displacement). Rehearsal observation is adjacent — hunters can watch from wings or stalls.

nodes.label: ARTISTIC DIRECTOR'S OFFICE
nodes.player_desc: Marcus Yuen's office. Unlocked — he left for a board meeting and didn't come back.
nodes.keeper_note: Route C. Grant documentation here: Solstice Property Group, acquisition appendix (prompt book), performance continuity condition. MESA internal document misfiled among financial records. Marcus returns in Scene 2 — Alan's Agatha Dell seed happens here if pursued.

nodes.label: LOADING BAY
nodes.player_desc: The back of the theatre. Two vehicles, four people in dark jackets. They've been here for an hour.
nodes.keeper_note: Scene 3. Rook is here. Three-phase confrontation: negotiation, the arrays, the offer. BIM Collection Arrays are in lighting rig above stage — accessible from here. Where the MESA thread becomes physical.

edges.label: Edmund explains the mechanism — investigation moves here
Prompt book listed in grant acquisition
Clara has been watching MESA for months
Rehearsal reveals BIM arrays in rig
Financial documents confirm MESA funded the theatre
